news.title: US is in for 'hot and sticky' inflation this summer: Economist on Fed, PCE
news.summary: May's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index — the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge — saw inflation rise to its highest level since 2023. The measure ticked up 0.4% month-over-month and 4.1% year-over-year, while core PCE — which excludes food and energy price volatility — rose 0.3% monthly and 3.4% annually. KPMG US chief economist Diane Swonk assesses the most recent wave of inflation data and what it signals for the US this summer, and whether these price trends change the Federal Reserve's strategy around interest rates at all.

news.title: BofA cuts euro forecasts, sees stronger dollar in second half on hawkish Fed
news.summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America has revised its G10 foreign-exchange forecasts for the second half of 2026, lowering its euro-dollar projections and adopting a more constructive near-term view on the U.S. dollar as resilient U.S. economic data and a hawkish Federal Reserve continue to support the greenback.
